chunk_summary: This section discusses counselling and home diet therapy for managing uncomplicated moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), focusing on feeding strategies and supplementary nutrition to address moderate wasting in children or adults.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of counselling for families managing moderate acute malnutrition (MAM).
Learn dietary recommendations and home-based interventions for uncomplicated moderate wasting.
Identify appropriate supplementary feeding strategies for MAM.
Recognize the importance of monitoring and follow-up in home diet therapy.
key_facts: Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) is characterized by weight-for-height Z-scores between -2 and -3 SD.
Home diet therapy emphasizes energy-dense, nutrient-rich foods tailored to local availability.
Counselling should focus on feeding frequency, portion sizes, and food diversity.
Supplementary feeding may include ready-to-use supplementary foods (RUSF) or fortified blended foods.
Regular follow-up is essential to monitor progress and prevent progression to severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
reference_quote: Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) can be effectively managed through a combination of counselling, home diet therapy, and supplementary feeding tailored to individual needs.
